title: RX callback not re-registered after disable/enable cycle
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c lines 248-265, lora_l2.c lines 324-325

When the L2 interface is disabled via `lichen_l2_enable(false)`, `lichen_lora_l2_stop()` clears the RX callback (lora_l2.c line 324-325). However, when re-enabled via `lichen_l2_enable(true)`, the callback is never re-registered:
- `lichen_l2_enable(true)` only re-initializes `link_ctx` and calls `lichen_lora_l2_start()`
- `lichen_lora_l2_start()` configures the radio and starts the RX thread but does not touch the callback

After a disable/enable cycle:
1. RX thread is running (created by `lichen_lora_l2_start()`)
2. `lora_state.rx_callback` is NULL (cleared by prior stop, never restored)
3. Packets are received and pass all checks in `rx_thread()`
4. But the callback check (`if (cb)` at lora_l2.c line 204) silently drops them

What breaks: After any disable/enable cycle, the node silently stops receiving packets. TX still works. No errors are logged. This is a silent data loss bug that would be very difficult to debug in the field.

Fix: Either:
1. Re-register the RX callback in `lichen_l2_enable(true)`:
   `lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(lora_rx_callback, NULL);`
2. Or do not clear the callback in `lichen_lora_l2_stop()` (but this has other implications for cleanup semantics)
